sections.name: Grusk The Pusk
sections.text:
While the guards who built this shrine are long dead, a pusk demon named Grusk has lurked here since the time they perished. Once a pawn of the shrine's priest, over the course of thousands of years slurping the supernatural blood and bullying the blood-red imps who occasionally manifest from the broken altar, the slothful pusk has achieved the unimaginable—it has grown more powerful than others of his ilk.   
  
Grusk isn't motivated to leave the room, which he considers his empire, in particular because new imps crawl from the broken altar every few years to replace the ones he kills. The imps clamber through a pinpoint of thinness between the Universe and the Outer Rifts, only to become stranded. They make do with their lot by lurking just out of Grusk's reach and picking on him until one inevitably lets down their guard long enough for Grusk to snatch it. Of course, all the fiends titter in delight upon spotting the PCs, and attack at once.

traits.name: Unique
traits.text: A rules element with this trait is one-of-a-kind. The DC of Recall Knowledge checks related to creatures with this trait is increased by 10.

traits.name: Demon
traits.text: A family of sinful fiends from the Outer Rifts.

traits.name: Fiend
traits.text: Creatures that hail from or have a strong connection to the unholy planes are called fiends. Fiends can survive the basic environmental effects of planes in the Outer Sphere.

traits.name: Unholy
traits.text: Effects with the unholy trait are tied to powerful magical forces of cruelty and sin. They often have stronger effects on holy creatures. Creatures with this trait are strongly devoted to unholy causes, and often have weakness to holy. If a creature with weakness to unholy uses an unholy item or effect, it takes damage from its weakness.

traits.name: Magical
traits.text: Something with the magical trait is imbued with magical energies not tied to a specific tradition of magic. Some items or effects are closely tied to a particular tradition of magic. In these cases, the item has the arcane, divine, occult, or primal trait instead of the magical trait. Any of these traits indicate that the item is magical.

traits.name: Unholy
traits.text: Effects with the unholy trait are tied to powerful magical forces of cruelty and sin. They often have stronger effects on holy creatures. Creatures with this trait are strongly devoted to unholy causes, and often have weakness to holy. If a creature with weakness to unholy uses an unholy item or effect, it takes damage from its weakness.

traits.name: Agile
traits.text: The multiple attack penalty you take with this weapon on the second attack on your turn is –4 instead of –5, and –8 instead of –10 on the third and subsequent attacks in the turn.

traits.name: Magical
traits.text: Something with the magical trait is imbued with magical energies not tied to a specific tradition of magic. Some items or effects are closely tied to a particular tradition of magic. In these cases, the item has the arcane, divine, occult, or primal trait instead of the magical trait. Any of these traits indicate that the item is magical.

traits.name: Unholy
traits.text: Effects with the unholy trait are tied to powerful magical forces of cruelty and sin. They often have stronger effects on holy creatures. Creatures with this trait are strongly devoted to unholy causes, and often have weakness to holy. If a creature with weakness to unholy uses an unholy item or effect, it takes damage from its weakness.

ability.name: Blood Slurp
ability.requirement: Grusk is adjacent to a creature taking persistent bleed damage
ability.effect: Grusk attempts a jaws Strike against the bleeding creature. If he hits, he inflicts normal damage but also swallows a gulp of the creature's blood. The creature becomes enfeebled 1 for 1 minute, and Grusk grows more energetic, losing his Sloth feature for 1 minute.

ability.name: Threaten Imp

traits.name: Auditory
traits.text: Auditory actions and effects rely on sound. An action with the auditory trait can be successfully performed only if the creature using the action can speak or otherwise produce the required sounds. A spell or effect with the auditory trait has its effect only if the target can hear it. This applies only to sound-based parts of the effect, as determined by the GM. This is different from a sonic effect, which still affects targets who can’t hear it (such as deaf targets) as long as the effect itself makes sound.

traits.name: Linguistic
traits.text: An effect with this trait depends on language comprehension. A linguistic effect that targets a creature works only if the target understands the language you are using.

ability.text: Grusk barks a threatening order to an imp within 30 feet. That imp can use its reaction to Stride or Strike.
